author: @Zach_TheGamer
text: This movie has NOTHING to do with Tron Legacy and Jeff Bridges is just a glorified cameo. They actually just ignore Tron Legacy. They bring back the Grid from the first movie just for member berries. We waited 15 years for a sequel to Tron Legacy. It should have been about the characters from Tron Legacy. Sam Flynn,  Quorra, Tron, and Kevin Flynn (yes, he died in Tron Legacy, but they could have found a way for Jeff Bridges to be a main part of the movie). This movie could have had a great story. They could have made a story about the ISOs (Isomorphic Algorithms). They appeared in the Grid in Tron Legacy. They spawned from nothing. They are living beings (not programs) inside of a digital space. They weren’t made, they manifested from out of knowhere. As Kevin Flynn said, “They were spectacular. Everything I'd hoped to find in the system—control, order, perfection—none of it meant a thing. I'd been living in a hall of mirrors. The ISOs shattered it. The possibilities of their root code, their digital DNA... Disease? History! Science, philosophy! Every idea man has ever had about the universe up for grabs! Bio-digital Jazz man!”.  They had incredible self generating healing abilities, but more than that, they have completely free will. They couldn’t be controlled by Kevin Flynn. They could have made an entire movie around this. They could have brought back the cast from Legacy. They could have had an amazing third movie. Instead, they just went for this flashy nostalgia trip mcguffin fetch quest. There is no substance to this movie. It’s not even really a sequel.

author: @marasmorgean5813
text:
Ok, I have to bring this up because I think Tron 2 deserves better...

I know a lot of people dislike the second movie but I think it actually carries a deeper meaning (unlike Tron 3, from what we have seen so far). It touches on questions like when something should be considered a lifeform and explores ideas around humanity, spirituality, morality and more.

If Im not mistaken, there are different types of programs in the second Tron movie. There is CLU (basically an artificial copy of Flynn) and then there are the ISOs. From what I remember the ISOs are not really considered AI like CLU is. They are more like digital lifeforms.

From Flynns perspective the ISOs are alive; and he is even willing to sacrifice himself to protect them.

Then there is CLU who is a literal copy of Flynn at a certain point in time. You could argue that when Flynn created CLU, he wasnt morally all that different from him. It was only later as time passed that Flynn began to change when he realized that everything was much bigger and more complex than he had originally imagined. I think the mirror imagery in the film symbolizes this very well and also the argument between Flynn and CLU before the betrayal. Flynn couldnt truly see CLU as an enemy because CLU was just doing what he was designed to do: build the perfect system (with all of Flynns earlier human flaws and assumptions baked in). Flynn himself would have failed in trying to create such a system and so CLU was always doomed to fail unless he changed too (which he couldnt).

DDoes it raise questions no one has ever asked before? No.
Does it provide good answers? Not really.
Does it need to provide answers at all? Thats up to you.

But I think it touches on seome important stuff; like doing the right thing - even if it means to take sacrifices, has a complete arc, a meaningful ending and gives you something to think about. Thats more than you can say for a lot of other movies released around the same time and something I never see anyone talking about when it comes to the 2nd movie.

replies.author: @joshcarter-com
replies.text: Tron Legacy had several layers of meaning that I think a lot of people missed. And, having just got back from watching Ares, I’m disappointed to report that Ares completely discards them. Even the ISOs are gone without explanation, and they would’ve been the perfect explanation for a program becoming more than its designer intended. Ares could’ve been a great movie, had it continued in the footsteps of Legacy, but apparently Disney had a different plan.

Little snippets of code telling them how long we stayed on the page, where the pointer was positioned, what we clicked on next, what page we came from, browser we used.... the list is endless and they want to know it all.
Won't be long before they use our webcams and phone cams to view what we're wearing and start sending us ads related to that.

Sorry I'll stop lol. 
I will say this though, if you showed a developer today some of the stuff that was programmed in the 80's and 90's they wouldn't have a clue about how it was done, for extra bewilderment you then tell them that it all fit on a 1.4MB floppy disk and just watch their mind melt in front of you lol.

Good times indeed.

author: @vegetasolo1221
text: There is so much to say about this movie but to start off omg I was not expecting this. This movie far exceeded my expectations! I went into this movie with little hope and as soon as the movie started I started to geek out with the little homages and Easter eggs I truly enjoyed watching this movie and I want to go back and watch it again to see what i missed. The story was so fun with the exploration of the grids but I think it was so amazing when we got to see the first grid from the original Tron movie! What a treat to see not only a throw back like that but to see that style again is so refreshing! The story is worth the watch without a doubt, don’t forget the incredible soundtrack that NIN did! My god it felt like the vibe of tron! With the previous movie using daft punk, NIN had some big shoes to fill but I assure you they did not disappoint. The only cons I would say in this movie  was mostly just the main female character felt very out of the blue I was really hoping we’d see someone familiar from the previous movies maybe using Sam, Kevin, even Alan or Cora it just felt a bit random to me but that’s not to say she didn’t do well because I think she was still a wonderful character! I was kinda bummed out that they didn’t really try to acknowledge the events of  Tron legacy in this movie as much because I really did love the vibe of that movie and I wish the could’ve evenly distributed the homage to both of the past movies while keeping it fresh. Other than that I did have trouble following the storyline of trying to figure out where they were or what was going on I just felt a bit lost at sometimes. The story felt like it for sure could have done a lot better but I still really liked it and has made me hopeful for a tron 4 after seeing how exciting the post credit scene was! I’m a huge fan of both the original tron and Tron: Legacy and I’d give this a 8/10 what a wonderful watch!!
